results.source_used: 1957_sl/CAA.pdf (Constitutional Amendments Approved), Chapter 396, "Korean Veterans Bonus Bond Issue," Article 65, § 1, approved June 26, 1956

findings.note: Stored body text matches the session-law text word-for-word. Session law reads (de-OCR'd) '§ 1.) The legislative assembly of the state of North Dakota is hereby authorized and empowered to provide by legislation for the issuance, sale and delivery of the bonds of the state of North Dakota in the principal amount not to exceed $9,000,000.00, the proceeds thereof to be used in payment of adjusted compensation to North Dakota veterans of the Korean conflict who served in the armed forces of the United States or any of its allies during the period from June 25, 1950 to July 27, 1953 on the basis of terms of service, and under such terms and conditions as the legislative assembly may prescribe.' Heading 'Korean Veterans Bonus Bond Issue' matches; Article 65 = amend. art. LXV; authority (HCR 'D', Ch. 358 1955 SL, enacted as Ch. 396 1957 SL, approved June 26, 1956) all confirmed against the CAA file. PDF OCR artifacts ('North D akota') are not in the stored text.

results.metadata_issues: None. Heading, article number, chapter authority, and approval date (June 26, 1956) all match the session law. The session law's note ('This measure was No. 1 on the primary election ballot') is consistent with the stored 1956-07-26 effective date; the session law itself states only the approval date, so the stored effective/election dates are neither confirmed nor contradicted by it.

findings.note: Amendment LXXIV's SOLE operative effect was to amend and reenact the THIRD paragraph of §215 to rename the institution. The session law enacts: 'Third: The North Dakota State University of Agriculture and Applied Science at the City of Fargo, in the County of Cass.' But the stored text's Third paragraph still reads the OLD, pre-amendment name: 'Third: The Agricultural College at the City of Fargo, in the County of Cass.' The stored record reproduces the entire unamended §215 and never applied the rename that this very amendment enacted, so the operative change is missing. (The stored heading correctly references 'ND State University of Agriculture,' but the body text contradicts it.) All other paragraphs of stored §215 are not part of this amendment's enacted text.
findings.sessionlaw_text:
Be It Enacted by the People of the State of North Dakota:

Paragraph third of section 215 of Article XIX of the Constitution of the state of North Dakota, as amended, is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows:

Third: The North Dakota State University of Agriculture and Applied Science at the City of Fargo, in the County of Cass.

results.source_used: 1965_sl/CMA.pdf (Constitutional Measures, Approved), Chapter 474, "MUNICIPAL JUDGES", § 1 Amendment of section 113; located by section number "113" and heading/distinctive wording "municipal judges"

findings.note: Body text is verbatim identical to the session law (SECTION 113 enacted text matches word-for-word). BUT the stored 'authority' field misidentifies the proposing resolution's chamber: stored says 'House Concurrent Resolution "T"' while the session law (ch. 474 heading) says 'Senate Concurrent Resolution "T", chapter 454, 1963 Session Laws'. Chapter (454) and letter ('T') match; chamber (House vs Senate) is wrong. Also the stored parenthetical '(mislabeled "T")' is unsupported by the session-law text, which cleanly cites Resolution "T". Effective date 1964-07-30 not contradicted: session law shows 'Approved June 30, 1964' (an OCR-truncated 'June 3' in the layout dump, but the parallel measures on the same primary ballot read 'June 30, 1964' and the measure note says it was measure No. 3 on the primary election ballot); July 30 effective date is the +30-day derived effective date, distinct from the June 30 election/approval date.
findings.sessionlaw_text:
CHAPTER 474

MUNICIPAL JUDGES

Senate Concurrent Resolution "T", chapter 454, 1963 Session Laws, proposed by the 38th Legislative Assembly of the state of North Dakota to provide for the amendment of section 113 of the Constitution of North Dakota, relating to the office [of] police magistrate by adding the words "selection or" before the word election; by adding the words "and the qualifications" after the word election; by omitting the words "police magistrates" and adding the words "municipal judges"; by omitting the words "in addition to their jurisdiction of all" and by adding the words "shall hear, try, and determine"; by omitting the words "shall be ex officio justices of the peace of the county in which said cities, towns and villages may be located. And the legislative assembly may confer upon said police magistrates the jurisdiction to hear, try, and determine all cases of misdemeanors, and the prosecutions therein shall be by information". And by adding the words "and shall have such other jurisdiction as the legislative assembly may confer upon them." so as to read as follows:

Be It Enacted by the People of the State of North Dakota:

§ 1. Amendment.) Section 113 of the Constitution of the state of North Dakota is hereby amended and reenacted to read as follows:

SECTION 113. The legislative assembly shall provide by law for the selection or election and the qualifications of municipal judges in cities, incorporated towns, and villages, who shall hear, try, and determine cases arising under the ordinances of said cities, towns and villages, and shall have such other jurisdiction as the legislative assembly may confer upon them.

Approved June 30, 1964.    55,202 to 49,504
Note: This was measure No. 3 on the primary election ballot.

results.metadata_issues: Authority chamber wrong: stored "House Concurrent Resolution \"T\"" vs session-law "Senate Concurrent Resolution \"T\", chapter 454, 1963 Session Laws". Chapter and letter ("T") are correct; only the chamber (House -> Senate) needs correction. The stored "(mislabeled \"T\")" parenthetical is unsupported by the session law and should be removed. Date: session law shows approval/election "June 30, 1964" (primary ballot measure No. 3); stored effective_date 1964-07-30 is consistent as the +30-day effective date and is not contradicted.

A superintendent of schools for each county shall be elected every four years beginning in the year 1964, whose qualifications, duties, powers and compensation shall be fixed by law. Provided, however, a superintendent of schools may be elected by and serve two or more counties or parts of counties as provided by law.

findings.sessionlaw_text:
A constitutional convention shall be called for the purpose of proposing a new Constitution, or revision or amendment of the existing Constitution. Such convention shall be called and conducted, and delegates thereto shall be chosen in the manner provided by law.

A constitutional convention called by a majority affirmative vote on this question shall be called and conducted as provided by law, and the proposed revision or amendment of the Constitution resulting from such shall be submitted by such convention directly to the people of North Dakota for approval or disapproval at the statewide special election called by the governor as provided by law.

results.metadata_issues: No substantive metadata errors. Note: the session-law chapter title is "CONSTITUTIONAL CONVENTION"; the stored heading is a descriptive paraphrase ("Calls for a constitutional convention") rather than the verbatim session-law title — not an error if descriptive headings are the convention. Effective date 1970-10-01 is consistent with primary-election approval Sept. 1, 1970; election_date is null but the session law shows this was on the primary election ballot (constitutional measure No. 2), approved Sept. 1, 1970 — consider populating election_date.

results.source_used: 1973_sl/CAA.pdf (Constitutional Amendments, Approved; Chapter 526, "Constitutional Status of UND-Ellendale Branch", HCR 3045; located by "Section 216" and "Ellendale"). CAP.pdf does not contain this amendment.

findings.note: The enacted body text matches in substance, but the stored text diverges from the session law in three places and (a) appends non-enacted Blue Book editorial annotation and (b) omits the second operative part of the measure. Specifics: (1) Stored 'McHenry, Ward, Bottineau or Rolette' vs session law 'McHenry, Ward, Bottineau, or Rolette' (session law has a comma after 'Bottineau'). (2) Stored 'in the county of Stark, (b)' (comma) vs session law 'in the county of Stark. (b)' (period). (3) Stored 'revision of this constitution' (lowercase) vs session law 'revision of this Constitution' (capital C). (4) Stored text appends editorial matter not in the enacted law: '(As amended by Articles 21 and 22 ... S.L. 1917, pp. 407 and 408.) Amendments: Art. 12, Nov. 8. 1910 ... Art. 90, Sept. 5. 1972 (S.L. 1971. ch. 623; S.L. 1973, ch. 526).' — this is Blue Book annotation, not enacted text. (5) The session law (Chapter 526, SECTION 2) ALSO amends and reenacts 'Subsection 1 of article 54 of the amendments' (State Board of Higher Education membership), which the stored record omits entirely.
findings.sessionlaw_text:
Section 216. The following named public institutions are hereby permanently located as hereinafter provided, each to have so much of the remaining grant of one hundred seventy thousand acres of land made by the United States for "other educational and charitable institutions" as is allotted by law, namely:

First: A soldiers' home, when located, or such other charitable institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at Lisbon, in the county of Ransom, with a grant of forty thousand acres of land.

Second: The blind asylum shall be known as the North Dakota school for the blind and may be removed from the county of Pembina to such other location as may be determined by the board of administration to be in the best interests of the students of such institution and the state of North Dakota.

Third: A school of forestry, or such other institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at such place in one of the counties of McHenry, Ward, Bottineau, or Rolette, as the electors of said counties may determine by an election for that purpose, to be held as provided by the legislative assembly.

Fourth: A scientific school or such other educational or charitable institution as the legislative assembly may prescribe, at the city of Wahpeton, county of Richland, with a grant of forty thousand acres.

Fifth: A state normal school at the city of Minot in the county of Ward.

Sixth: (a) A state normal school at the city of Dickinson, in the county of Stark. (b) A state hospital for the insane at such place within this state as shall be selected by the legislative assembly, provided, that no other institution of a character similar to any one of those located by this article shall be established or maintained without a revision of this Constitution.

results.metadata_issues: Heading: stored heading "State institutions (Ellendale removed)" is reasonable; the session law's chapter caption is "CONSTITUTIONAL STATUS OF UND-ELLENDALE BRANCH" (Chapter 526). Effective/election date: stored effective_date 1972-10-05 and authority's "approved at primary election ... September 5, 1972, 68,575 to 41,350; chapter 526" both match the session law ("Approved September 5, 1972. 68,575 to 41,350"). election_date is null in the stored record though the vote occurred Sept. 5, 1972. Scope/omission: the enacted measure (Chapter 526) amends BOTH section 216 (SECTION 1) and subsection 1 of article 54 (SECTION 2 — State Board of Higher Education); the stored record records only the § 216 change and omits the article 54 amendment.

findings.note: Stored text matches the session law verbatim. The only differences are OCR artifacts in the session-law PDF scan itself ('electo·rs' for 'electors', 'Section.74' for 'Section 74'); the stored text has the correct clean words. No word/number/structural difference.
findings.sessionlaw_text: Section 74. The governor and lieutenant governor shall be elected upon a joint ballot by the qualified electors of the state beginning with elections held in 1976. A single vote shall be cast upon a joint ballot by each qualified elector for the joint candidates representing the political party or affiliation of his choice. The joint candidates having the highest number of votes shall be declared elected, but if two or more joint candidates shall have an equal and highest number of votes for governor and lieutenant governor, the two houses of the legislative assembly at its next regular session shall forthwith in joint session choose one pair of such joint candidates for said offices. The returns of the election for governor and lieutenant governor shall be made in such manner as shall be prescribed by law.

findings.note: Stored text matches the session law verbatim. Only differences are OCR artifacts in the session-law PDF scan ('unless '·they' for 'unless they', 'prescribed bY' for 'prescribed by'); the stored text has the correct clean words. No word/number/structural difference.
findings.sessionlaw_text: Section 77. The powers and duties of the lieutenant governor shall be to serve as president of the senate, but he shall have no vote unless they be equally divided. Additional duties shall be prescribed by the governor. If, during a vacancy in the office of governor, the lieutenant governor shall be impeached, displaced, resign or die, or from mental or physical disease, or otherwise become incapable of performing the duties of his office, the secretary of state shall act as governor until the vacancy shall be filled or the disability removed.

results.source_used: 1975_sl/CAA.pdf, Constitutional Amendments Approved, Chapter 603, "SIZE OF TRIAL JURIES", §7 amendment (HCR 3002, ch. 532, 1973)

findings.note: Stored body text matches the CAA.pdf enacted text verbatim, word for word and punctuation for punctuation. Heading 'Size of Trial Juries' matches the chapter caption 'SIZE OF TRIAL JURIES'. Effective date July 1, 1975 matches Section 2 of the session law. Authority (HCR 3002, ch. 532, 1973; approved at the 1974 primary) matches the chapter header and note.
findings.sessionlaw_text: Section 7. The right of trial by jury shall be secured to all, and remain inviolate. A person accused of a crime for which he may be confined for a period of more than one year has the right of trial by a jury of twelve. The legislative assembly may determine the size of the jury for all other cases, provided that the jury consists of at least six members. All verdicts must be unanimous.

findings.note: Stored text matches the session law word-for-word, including punctuation and numbers. Session law (CHAPTER 692, SECTION 1) reads: 'Section 65. No bill shall become a law: except by a vote of a majority of all the members-elect in the house of representatives, and a vote of the majority of the members-elect in the senate, however the lieutenant governor may vote as provided in section 77 in the event the senate is equally divided, nor unless, on its final passage, the vote be taken by yeas and nays, and the names of those voting be entered on the journal.' Only difference vs stored is OCR layout whitespace, which is normalized.

Stored text matches the session law word-for-word. Session law (CHAPTER 692, SECTION 2) reads: 'Section 77. The powers and duties of the lieutenant governor shall be to serve as president of the senate, and he may, when the senate is equally divided, vote on procedural matters, and on substantive matters if his vote would be decisive. Additional duties shall be prescribed by the governor. If, during the vacancy in the office of governor, the lieutenant governor shall be impeached, displaced, resign, or die, or from mental or physical disease, or otherwise become incapable of performing the duties of his office, the secretary of state shall act as governor until the vacancy shall be filled or the disability removed.' Only differences vs stored are OCR layout spacing (e.g. 'decisive .' / 'office ,'), normalized away.

Correct effective date for the new article AND for the repeals of § 25 and art. XXXIII is 1979-01-01, not 1978-12-07. results.source_used: 1981_sl/CAA.pdf, Constitutional Amendments, Approved, Chapter 654 "STATE PROPERTY TAX AUTHORITY," §174 amendment (HCR 3009; approved Sept. 2, 1980, primary measure No. 2, 63,699 to 56,618)

findings.note: The session law strikes the old four-mill-levy language (rendered as OCR-garbled tilde/dash strikethrough: '~~ev~ae-fe~', 'e~ff~e~eR~', 'fe~--eaea--yea~...') and enacts new text. The only difference is the session-law scan prints 'section 174' in lowercase (an OCR/typesetting artifact in the source itself); words, numbers, and structure are identical.

results.source_used: 1981_sl/CAA.pdf (Chapter 655, Constitutional Amendments, Approved; "JUDICIAL OFFICERS"; SCR No. 4006; located via grep for "Section 173" and "subsection 6 of section 69"; approved Sept 2, 1980, 58,827 to 50,643)

findings.note: Body text is IDENTICAL to the clean amended session-law text (the OCR-garbled spans like 'eeaaty-;aa§e' and the long '~~ev~aea...' run are struck-through deletions, not part of the operative new text). The only problem is the effective date. The stored effective_date is 1980-10-02, but session-law SECTION 3 expressly provides a SEPARATE, LATER effective date for the §173 amendment: 'The provisions of section 1 of this resolution, if approved by the people, shall take effect on January 1, 1983.' Section 1 is the §173 amendment. Only the §69 repeal and the clerk-election provisions (sections 2 and 3) 'shall take effect thirty days after certification of approval' (i.e., ~1980-10-02). Applying 1980-10-02 to the §173 amendment is wrong; it should be 1983-01-01.
findings.sessionlaw_text: Section 173. There shall be elected in each county, organized under the provisions of section 172 of the Constitution of the State of North Dakota, a register of deeds, county auditor, treasurer, sheriff, state's attorney, and a clerk of the district court, who shall be electors in the county in which they are elected and who shall hold their office for a term of four years and until their successors are elected and qualified; provided that in counties having population of six thousand or less the register of deeds shall also be clerk of the district court. This amendment shall be self-executing, but legislation may be enacted to facilitate its operation.

findings.note: Session-law SECTION 2 REPEAL: 'Subsection 6 of section 69 of the Constitution of the State of North Dakota is hereby repealed.' Stored content '[Repealed effective 1980-10-02]' and heading match. Effective date 1980-10-02 is correct for this provision (sections 2 and 3 'shall take effect thirty days after certification of approval'; approved Sept 2, 1980).

results.metadata_issues: Split effective dates not reflected in stored record. The §173 amendment (SECTION 1 of SCR 4006) is effective January 1, 1983 per session-law SECTION 3, NOT 1980-10-02. Recommend recording the §173 change with effective_date 1983-01-01 while keeping the §69 repeal at 1980-10-02. Also: election_date is null in the stored record but the measure was approved at the September 2, 1980 primary (NOTE: "measure No. 5 on the primary election ballot"); election_date should be 1980-09-02.
